g.text: HTTP/1.1 Response Wire Format — Byte Anatomy (server.c)
Contiguous Byte Sequence (no framing — plain text on the wire)
while total_sent < len | loop until all bytes sent
n = send(fd, buf+sent, len-sent, MSG_NOSIGNAL) | one kernel call
n < 0 → return -1 | EPIPE if client closed; MSG_NOSIGNAL no SIGPIPE
n == 0 → return -1 | should not occur on blocking socket
return total_sent | all len bytes delivered to kernel send buffer
byte 16 | 0x0A | LF (\n) ← end of status line
HEADER: Content-Length ← MUST equal exact body byte count
| | body is exactly 27 bytes; mismatch → client hangs
0x0D | 0x0D | CR — this CRLF is the empty line
0x0A | 0x0A | LF — combined with last header CRLF = \r\n\r\n
parser searches for \r\n\r\n to find header/body boundary
Total body | 27 B | MUST match Content-Length: 27 header value
No length prefix | entire message has no outer frame
No type tag | receiver must parse text to find boundaries
CRLF each header | every header line ends 0x0D 0x0A
\r\n\r\n boundary | last-header CRLF + blank-line CRLF = separator
Content-Length | must equal strlen(body) in bytes, not chars
Too-small CL | client truncates response body early
Too-large CL | client hangs waiting for bytes never sent
No body for HEAD | headers identical to GET; body suppressed
send_all() loop | partial write possible; must loop until len==0
Total on wire | 130 B | contiguous; sent via send_all()
next byte — client parser knows body starts here
